methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Ares Industries is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Alex Tseng and Devan Plantamura, currently valued at $150M post-money on $31M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and NODA AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $150M Series A post-money valuation, implying $300M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and NODA AI ($125M)—yields a median of $300M and range of $125M–$500M. Both methods converge on identical implied values, strengthening the result.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M–$345M, with a median of $300M across the two applicable methods. The analysis supports a fair value midpoint 100% above the current $150M valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue traction and customer concentration relative to the peer set, where the $375M spread between peer outliers (Stark Defence versus NODA AI) reflects material valuation dispersion even within tightly filtered comparables. Stage-progression relies on typical multipliers that may not reflect Ares-specific risk or growth profile.
